channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026 in Ghana: Capacity Building, Mentorship and Grant Funding for Youth-Led Organizations
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Youth-led organizations and social enterprises in Ghana now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their impact through the SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026, an initiative under the EU-Ghana Youth Empowerment Programme. Implemented by SOS Children’s Villages Ghana in partnership with the European Union and other collaborating organizations, the programme is designed to empower youth-led and youth-focused […]

The post SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026 in Ghana: Capacity Building, Mentorship and Grant Funding for Youth-Led Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Youth-led organizations and social enterprises in Ghana now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their impact through the SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026, an initiative under the EU-Ghana Youth Empowerment Programme.

Implemented by SOS Children’s Villages Ghana in partnership with the European Union and other collaborating organizations, the programme is designed to empower youth-led and youth-focused civil society organizations (CSOs) and social enterprises with the knowledge, skills, mentorship, and technical support they need to expand their impact and contribute to sustainable development.

Beyond training and organizational development, the programme also provides successful participants with the opportunity to compete for grant funding, enabling them to scale their projects and create lasting change within their communities.

If your organization is passionate about empowering young people, promoting innovation, protecting the environment, or strengthening communities, this programme offers an excellent platform to build capacity and access future funding opportunities.

About the SPARK Change-Makers Project

The SPARK Change-Makers Project is a comprehensive organizational development programme that seeks to strengthen youth-led organizations before providing access to financial support.

Rather than offering immediate grants, the programme adopts a phased approach, ensuring participating organizations first receive intensive capacity-building support to improve their effectiveness, governance, sustainability, and long-term impact.

Selected organizations will participate in a structured programme that equips them with practical skills, strategic guidance, and expert support before becoming eligible to compete for grant funding.

The initiative focuses on empowering young people between the ages of 18 and 35 by strengthening the organizations that serve and represent them.

Programme Benefits

Organizations selected for the SPARK Change-Makers Project will receive a wide range of support designed to strengthen their operations and increase their impact.

Benefits include:

  • Comprehensive organizational capacity development.
  • Leadership and organizational skills training.
  • One-on-one coaching from experienced professionals.
  • Expert mentorship throughout the programme.
  • Technical assistance and advisory support.
  • Networking opportunities with partners and stakeholders.
  • Access to future grant funding opportunities.
  • Support for developing sustainable income-generating activities.
  • Improved organizational sustainability and long-term growth.
  • Increased ability to deliver impactful community projects.

Opportunity to Access Grant Funding

One of the major highlights of the SPARK Change-Makers Project is the opportunity to access grant funding.

Organizations that successfully complete the programme’s capacity-strengthening phase become eligible to compete for grants that can help expand and scale their initiatives.

This ensures that participating organizations are better prepared to manage funding effectively while maximizing community impact.

Who Can Apply?

The programme is open to organizations that are youth-led or youth-focused and operate within the programme’s six implementing regions of Ghana.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Youth-led civil society organizations (CSOs).
  • Youth-focused civil society organizations.
  • Youth-led social enterprises.
  • Youth-focused social enterprises.

Priority Focus Areas

The SPARK Change-Makers Project supports organizations working in sectors that contribute to youth empowerment and sustainable development.

Priority areas include:

1. Gender Empowerment

Projects that promote gender equality, women’s empowerment, inclusion, and equal opportunities for young people.

2. Agriculture and Environmental Protection

Initiatives focused on sustainable agriculture, climate resilience, environmental conservation, food security, and green innovation.

3. Peace and Security

Projects that promote peaceful communities, conflict prevention, civic participation, and social cohesion.

4. Culture and Creative Industries

Organizations supporting arts, culture, creative entrepreneurship, and cultural preservation among young people.

5. Digital Technology and Innovation

Projects leveraging digital tools, technology, innovation, and entrepreneurship to improve lives and create opportunities for youth.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on several important factors.

Strong applications should demonstrate:

  • Youth leadership within the organization.
  • Clear organizational goals and objectives.
  • Active community engagement.
  • Commitment to inclusion and diversity.
  • Environmental sustainability.
  • Potential for long-term impact.
  • Organizational readiness for growth and scaling.

Organizations that clearly demonstrate these qualities will be well positioned during the selection process.

Why This Programme Matters

Many youth-led organizations possess innovative ideas and strong community connections but often face challenges such as limited organizational capacity, inadequate technical support, and restricted access to funding.

The SPARK Change-Makers Project addresses these challenges by investing in organizational development before providing access to financial resources.

Participants gain practical knowledge, professional guidance, and stronger institutional capacity, enabling them to build sustainable organizations that continue delivering impact long after the programme ends.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 31 July 2026

Interested organizations should complete and submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the programme.

Click here for more and to apply

Why You Should Apply

If your organization is committed to creating positive change for young people in Ghana, the SPARK Change-Makers Project offers far more than funding. It provides the skills, mentorship, networks, and institutional support needed to build a stronger, more sustainable organization capable of delivering lasting impact.

Whether you work in gender equality, environmental sustainability, peacebuilding, the creative economy, or digital innovation, this programme can help you strengthen your organization while positioning it for future grant opportunities.

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026 in Ghana: Capacity Building, Mentorship and Grant Funding for Youth-Led Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Women Opens Applications for the Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab Philippines 2026 on Gender-Responsive and Inclusive Skills Development
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women), in partnership with the German Agency for International Cooperation (GIZ) through the Regional Cooperation on Labour Market Policy and Technical and Vocational Education and Training for a Twin Transition in ASEAN (RECOTwin) project, has officially opened applications for the Women’s Empowerment […]

The post UN Women Opens Applications for the Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab Philippines 2026 on Gender-Responsive and Inclusive Skills Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women), in partnership with the German Agency for International Cooperation (GIZ) through the Regional Cooperation on Labour Market Policy and Technical and Vocational Education and Training for a Twin Transition in ASEAN (RECOTwin) project, has officially opened applications for the Women’s Empowerment Principles (WEPs) Corporate Action Lab Philippines 2026.

Applications are open from 15 July to 15 August 2026.

This six-month innovation programme is designed for businesses that are ready to strengthen gender equality while building a future-ready workforce capable of thriving in the country’s rapidly expanding green and digital economy.

The initiative comes at a critical time as the Philippines undergoes its green and digital “Twin Transition,” creating new employment opportunities while also exposing significant skills shortages across multiple sectors. At the same time, many talented individuals—including women, young people, LGBTQIA+ persons, people with disabilities and other underrepresented groups—continue to experience barriers that prevent them from accessing quality skills development and employment opportunities.

The WEPs Corporate Action Lab seeks to help businesses address these challenges by developing innovative, practical and measurable solutions that promote inclusive skills development and equitable career pathways.

About the Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab

The Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab (WEPsCAL) is a cohort-based programme that supports companies over a period of approximately six months.

Rather than offering theoretical discussions, the programme functions as an innovation laboratory where participating companies design, pilot, evaluate and improve real workplace solutions tailored to their own organizational needs.

Participants receive expert guidance throughout the programme while collaborating with other businesses that are equally committed to promoting gender equality and inclusive employment.

The programme is implemented under the UN Women Gender Action Lab (GAL): Innovation and Impact for Gender Equality in the Asia-Pacific, in partnership with Australia’s Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade.

Why Inclusive Skills Development Matters

The Philippines is experiencing rapid transformation driven by green technologies and digital innovation.

Although these developments are creating thousands of new career opportunities, employers continue to report significant challenges in finding workers with the required skills.

At the same time, structural inequalities continue to prevent many talented individuals from accessing technical education, vocational training and quality employment.

UN Women believes that businesses can address these challenges while simultaneously improving productivity, innovation and long-term competitiveness by embedding Gender Equality, Disability and Social Inclusion (GEDSI) principles into workplace learning and career development.

Gender-responsive and inclusive training programmes can help businesses:

  • Build stronger talent pipelines.
  • Improve employee retention.
  • Increase productivity.
  • Encourage workplace innovation.
  • Develop a workforce prepared for future labour market demands.
  • Support sustainable economic growth.

Focus of the 2026 Corporate Action Lab

The selected companies will work together to answer the following challenge:

How can businesses design and implement skills development initiatives that create equitable career pathways for women and diverse talent during the green and digital transitions?

Throughout the programme, participants will receive technical support to develop practical solutions that can be tested within their organizations.

Objectives of the Programme

The Corporate Action Lab will support participating companies to:

  1. Analyse the business case for gender-responsive training by demonstrating its financial and operational value.
  2. Identify and eliminate unconscious bias within corporate training materials, learning environments and delivery approaches.
  3. Remove systemic barriers preventing women and diverse learners from entering technical and emerging occupations.
  4. Design, pilot and evaluate innovative workplace solutions tailored to their specific organizational contexts.
  5. Develop measurable Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to monitor progress and sustain inclusive training standards.
  6. Share successful practices with stakeholders across the Philippines and ASEAN to encourage wider policy adoption and scaling.

Benefits of Participating

Selected companies will receive numerous benefits throughout the programme, including:

  • Access to the Women’s Empowerment Principles Gender Gap Analysis Tool.
  • Use of exclusive WEPs resources.
  • Development of customized organizational workplans.
  • Individual technical assistance and coaching sessions.
  • Innovation and problem-solving support.
  • Peer learning with other participating businesses.
  • Networking opportunities with experts and solution providers.
  • Access to innovators working on gender equality and inclusive employment.
  • National recognition.
  • Regional recognition.
  • Global recognition upon successful completion of the programme.

Number of Companies to be Selected

UN Women plans to select 8–12 companies for the 2026 Philippines Corporate Action Lab.

Information Sessions

Interested organizations are encouraged to attend one of the free online information sessions before submitting their applications.

The sessions will explain:

  • Programme requirements
  • Eligibility criteria
  • Benefits of participation
  • Application process
  • Selection process

Information session dates include:

  • 22 July 2026
  • 29 July 2026
  • 10 August 2026

Each session will be held online via Microsoft Teams.

Application Process

Interested companies should complete the official online application form before the deadline.

After applications are reviewed, UN Women will contact successful applicants via email.

Successful participants are expected to be notified in early September 2026, while the official programme launch is tentatively scheduled for the second week of September 2026.

Application Deadline

15 August 2026

How to Apply

Eligible companies should complete the official online application form through the UN Women application portal before the deadline.

For additional information, applicants are encouraged to review the Challenge Brief, Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs), and attend one of the scheduled information sessions before submitting their applications.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more Global Training Opportunities here.

The post UN Women Opens Applications for the Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab Philippines 2026 on Gender-Responsive and Inclusive Skills Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SEACAF 2026 Research Fellowship: Receive Up to USD 15,000 to Advance Academic Freedom Research in Southeast Asia
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Southeast Asia Coalition for Academic Freedom (SEACAF) has officially opened applications for its 2026 Research Fellowships on Empowering Academic Freedom in Southeast Asia, offering funding of up to USD 15,000 for research projects that address some of the most pressing challenges facing higher education and academic freedom across the region. This prestigious fellowship is […]

The post SEACAF 2026 Research Fellowship: Receive Up to USD 15,000 to Advance Academic Freedom Research in Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Southeast Asia Coalition for Academic Freedom (SEACAF) has officially opened applications for its 2026 Research Fellowships on Empowering Academic Freedom in Southeast Asia, offering funding of up to USD 15,000 for research projects that address some of the most pressing challenges facing higher education and academic freedom across the region.

This prestigious fellowship is designed to support early-career researchers, academics, and research advocates committed to producing evidence-based research that strengthens academic freedom, promotes democratic values, and informs policy reforms throughout Southeast Asia.

Each year, the programme selects six fellows who will participate in a structured fellowship lasting between 10 and 12 months, combining financial support, expert mentorship, peer learning, and opportunities to contribute meaningful policy recommendations that can influence higher education systems across the region.

About the SEACAF Research Fellowship

Academic freedom remains one of the foundations of quality higher education. It enables scholars, educators, and students to freely teach, research, publish, and engage in critical inquiry without fear of censorship or retaliation.

Across Southeast Asia, however, academic freedom faces increasing pressure from democratic backsliding, government restrictions, institutional control, and broader political challenges.

Academics and students who engage in research or public discussions on social justice, governance, human rights, and public policy increasingly encounter obstacles such as:

  • State surveillance
  • Administrative sanctions
  • Restrictions on teaching and research
  • Criminalization of dissent
  • Limitations on publishing academic work
  • Political interference in universities
  • Constraints arising from national security policies
  • Increased risks in conflict-affected areas

The SEACAF Research Fellowship seeks to generate high-quality research that helps governments, universities, civil society organizations, and policymakers better understand these challenges while developing practical solutions that strengthen academic freedom across Southeast Asia.

Fellowship Themes

Applicants are invited to submit research proposals under one of three priority themes.

Theme 1: Neoliberal Higher Education and Its Impacts on Academic Freedom

Across Southeast Asia, universities increasingly operate under market-driven models that emphasize:

  • Revenue generation
  • Competition
  • Commercialization
  • International rankings
  • Performance indicators

While these reforms aim to improve institutional competitiveness, they may also weaken:

  • Academic independence
  • Institutional autonomy
  • Research integrity
  • Public service missions of universities
  • Freedom of teaching and inquiry

Research under this theme should use practical case studies to examine how neoliberal higher education policies affect academic freedom in real-world settings.

The findings are expected to contribute to policy recommendations that promote universities as spaces for independent thinking, critical knowledge production, and public engagement.

Funding: Up to USD 12,000

Duration: 10 months

Application Type: Individual or team

Theme 2: Developing Regional Indicators for Academic Freedom

Measuring academic freedom requires reliable and context-specific indicators.

This fellowship theme supports research that works closely with stakeholders across Southeast Asia to develop:

  • Regional academic freedom indicators
  • Monitoring frameworks
  • Documentation systems
  • Context-sensitive assessment tools
  • Regional policy recommendations

The long-term objective is to establish a comprehensive academic freedom monitoring system throughout Southeast Asia.

Funding: Up to USD 12,000

Duration: 10 months

Application Type: Individual or team

Theme 3: Participatory Action Research for Training Modules on Academic Freedom

This theme focuses on developing practical educational resources that strengthen understanding of academic freedom among universities and student communities.

Research teams will collaborate directly with academic institutions, educators, and students to:

  • Develop training modules
  • Produce educational materials
  • Test learning resources through participatory workshops
  • Improve awareness of academic freedom

Unlike the other themes, this fellowship requires collaborative research.

Funding: Up to USD 15,000

Duration: 12 months

Application Type: Team application only (minimum of three members)

Fellowship Benefits

Successful fellows will receive much more than financial support.

Benefits include:

  • Funding of up to USD 15,000 depending on the fellowship theme.
  • Participation in a 10–12 month structured fellowship.
  • Expert mentorship from experienced researchers and practitioners.
  • Peer learning with researchers from across Southeast Asia.
  • Opportunities to produce impactful research.
  • Development of policy recommendations for governments and higher education institutions.
  • Contribution to strengthening academic freedom across the region.
  • Increased professional visibility and networking opportunities.

What Can the Fellowship Funding Cover?

The fellowship provides flexible funding to support research activities.

Eligible expenses include:

Researcher’s Allowance

  • Monthly stipend or compensation for researchers throughout the fellowship period.

Research Expenses

Funding may be used for costs directly associated with conducting research.

Fieldwork and Travel

Support includes:

  • Transportation
  • Accommodation
  • Field visits
  • Other fieldwork-related costs

Research Assistants

Funding may cover payments for research assistants supporting project implementation.

Data Collection

Eligible costs include:

  • Surveys
  • Interviews
  • Transcription services
  • Data acquisition
  • Other research-related data collection activities

Why Academic Freedom Matters

Academic freedom allows universities to fulfill their public mission by encouraging independent thinking, innovation, and evidence-based research.

Without academic freedom:

  • Research quality declines.
  • Public debate becomes limited.
  • Universities lose institutional independence.
  • Innovation and creativity suffer.
  • Democratic participation weakens.

The SEACAF fellowship seeks to strengthen higher education systems where scholars can freely pursue knowledge while contributing to more democratic and inclusive societies.

Selection Information

For the 2026 fellowship cohort:

  • Only six fellows will be selected.
  • Proposals aligned with the three priority themes will receive the highest consideration.
  • Proposals outside these themes may still be submitted but will receive lower priority during the review process.

Research Duration

Depending on the selected theme, fellows will conduct research over:

  • 10 months (Themes 1 and 2)
  • 12 months (Theme 3)

Throughout the fellowship, participants will receive structured support while conducting original research and producing policy-oriented outputs.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 20 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their proposals early and submit all required documents before the deadline.

Click here for more and to apply

Why You Should Apply

If you are passionate about higher education, human rights, democratic governance, or public policy in Southeast Asia, the SEACAF Research Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to conduct meaningful research while influencing regional policy discussions.

Beyond funding, fellows become part of a growing regional network committed to protecting academic freedom, strengthening higher education institutions, and ensuring that universities remain places where knowledge, critical inquiry, and truth can flourish.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

 

The post SEACAF 2026 Research Fellowship: Receive Up to USD 15,000 to Advance Academic Freedom Research in Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the 2027 Young Strategists Forum by the German Marshall Fund of the United States: A Global Fellowship for Emerging Strategic Leaders in Indo-Pacific Security
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The 2027 Young Strategists Forum (YSF) by the German Marshall Fund of the United States (GMF) is now accepting applications from emerging leaders who are interested in strengthening their strategic thinking skills and contributing to discussions on international security, diplomacy, and global affairs. The Young Strategists Forum is a prestigious leadership development program designed to […]

The post Apply for the 2027 Young Strategists Forum by the German Marshall Fund of the United States: A Global Fellowship for Emerging Strategic Leaders in Indo-Pacific Security appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2027 Young Strategists Forum (YSF) by the German Marshall Fund of the United States (GMF) is now accepting applications from emerging leaders who are interested in strengthening their strategic thinking skills and contributing to discussions on international security, diplomacy, and global affairs.

The Young Strategists Forum is a prestigious leadership development program designed to prepare the next generation of strategic thinkers for a world facing increasingly complex geopolitical challenges, limited resources, and evolving security dynamics. The program provides participants with a unique platform to engage with experts, policymakers, diplomats, academics, and fellow emerging leaders from across the world.

Funded by and conducted in partnership with the Sasakawa Peace Foundation, the program focuses on strategic issues surrounding the United States-Japan alliance and security dynamics in the Indo-Pacific region. Through intensive learning activities, simulations, expert discussions, and networking opportunities, participants gain practical experience in analyzing international challenges and developing innovative policy solutions.

About the Young Strategists Forum

Since its establishment in March 2012, the Young Strategists Forum has developed into a highly respected platform for cultivating future foreign policy and security leaders. The program has created a strong international network of professionals who continue to influence policy discussions and strategic decision-making in their respective fields.

The fellowship is built around the idea that today’s global challenges require leaders who can think strategically, collaborate across borders, and understand the connections between security, economics, technology, and diplomacy.

The 2027 edition will continue this mission by bringing together talented professionals from different sectors and regions to examine emerging geopolitical issues and explore pathways toward international cooperation.

Program Focus: Strategy, Competition, and Cooperation in the Indo-Pacific

The Young Strategists Forum places significant emphasis on the changing security environment in the Indo-Pacific region. Participants will explore major global issues affecting international relations, including:

  • The future of the United States-Japan alliance
  • Regional security challenges in the Indo-Pacific
  • International cooperation and strategic competition
  • The role of emerging leaders in shaping foreign policy
  • Long-term approaches to global stability and security

The program encourages participants to understand complex global issues from multiple perspectives and develop strategic approaches to addressing international challenges.

Fellowship Activities and Program Format

The Young Strategists Forum provides a comprehensive learning experience combining academic discussions, practical exercises, and professional networking.

Selected participants will take part in activities including:

1. Expert Lectures and Strategic Discussions

Participants engage with leading experts, academics, diplomats, and policymakers who provide insights into international affairs, security policy, and strategic decision-making.

2. 36-Hour Simulation Exercise

One of the central components of the program is an intensive simulation exercise designed to challenge participants’ analytical skills, teamwork abilities, and strategic decision-making under pressure.

Through this exercise, participants experience realistic policy scenarios and develop solutions to complex international problems.

3. Meetings with Global Leaders

Participants have opportunities to interact with:

  • Policymakers
  • Diplomats
  • Senior journalists
  • Leading academics
  • Strategic experts

These discussions allow fellows to gain firsthand perspectives on global affairs and leadership challenges.

4. Study Tour Experience

The program also includes a study tour featuring visits to important institutions, including a military facility, allowing participants to gain practical understanding of security and defense issues.

Who Can Apply for the Young Strategists Forum?

The program is open to emerging leaders between the ages of 28 and 42 who demonstrate professional experience and interest in strategic issues.

Eligible applicants may include:

  • Academics
  • Journalists
  • Policymakers
  • Politicians
  • Business professionals
  • Military officers

Applicants should demonstrate leadership potential, professional achievement, and an interest in international affairs, security, and strategic policy discussions.

Eligible Countries and Regions

The fellowship welcomes applicants from:

  • The United States
  • European countries
  • Japan
  • Other like-minded Asian countries

The program aims to build a diverse international network of professionals who can collaborate across regions and contribute to global strategic discussions.

Benefits of Participating in the Young Strategists Forum

Successful participants will gain several valuable opportunities, including:

1. Leadership Development

Participants strengthen their ability to analyze complex challenges, develop strategic solutions, and lead discussions on international issues.

2. Global Professional Network

The fellowship connects participants with a global community of emerging leaders, experts, policymakers, and alumni working in strategic fields.

Since its creation, 162 individuals have participated in the Young Strategists Forum, with many alumni advancing into influential positions within their professions.

3. Exposure to International Policy Discussions

Participants gain direct exposure to discussions involving global security, diplomacy, foreign policy, and international cooperation.

4. Practical Strategic Skills

Through simulations and expert-led sessions, fellows develop practical skills in:

  • Strategic analysis
  • Decision-making
  • Policy development
  • International collaboration
  • Crisis management

5. Access to a Prestigious Alumni Network

Graduates become part of a growing network of professionals who continue engaging in global policy discussions and strategic initiatives.

Application Deadline

The application process for the 2027 Young Strategists Forum has two important deadlines:

  • New registrations must be completed by Thursday, August 13, 2026, at 11:59 AM ET / 17:59 CEST.
  • Completed applications must be submitted by Friday, August 14, 2026, at 11:59 AM ET / 17:59 CEST.

Applications submitted after these deadlines will not be accepted.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official application portal:

APPLY HERE

Applicants experiencing login or application issues can contact the program team through the provided contact information:
JPallanch@gmfus.org

Final Thoughts

The Young Strategists Forum represents a valuable opportunity for emerging leaders who want to expand their understanding of global security challenges and develop the strategic skills needed in today’s rapidly changing world.

By combining expert discussions, simulations, international networking, and leadership development, the fellowship equips participants with the knowledge and connections needed to contribute meaningfully to future policy conversations.

Professionals interested in international affairs, diplomacy, security studies, and strategic leadership are encouraged to prepare their applications before the deadline.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities here.

The post Apply for the 2027 Young Strategists Forum by the German Marshall Fund of the United States: A Global Fellowship for Emerging Strategic Leaders in Indo-Pacific Security appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: British Council Travel Grant Fund 2026: Funding for UK Filmmakers and XR Creatives to Attend International Festivals, Labs and Co-Production Forums
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The British Council Travel Grant Fund is an exciting funding opportunity designed to help filmmakers and XR (Extended Reality) creatives from the United Kingdom expand their careers by participating in leading international festivals, showcase events, creative development labs, and co-production forums. Supported through BFI National Lottery funding, in partnership with BFI NETWORK and the BFI […]

The post British Council Travel Grant Fund 2026: Funding for UK Filmmakers and XR Creatives to Attend International Festivals, Labs and Co-Production Forums appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The British Council Travel Grant Fund is an exciting funding opportunity designed to help filmmakers and XR (Extended Reality) creatives from the United Kingdom expand their careers by participating in leading international festivals, showcase events, creative development labs, and co-production forums.

Supported through BFI National Lottery funding, in partnership with BFI NETWORK and the BFI International Fund, this initiative aims to strengthen the global presence of UK creative talent by making international networking and showcasing opportunities more accessible.

The fund recognizes that attending major international industry events can significantly influence a filmmaker’s career. Whether presenting a short film, screening a feature film, showcasing an immersive XR project, or seeking production partners for a new feature, the Travel Grant Fund provides financial support that enables creatives to represent their work on the international stage.

Unlike many funding opportunities that operate under fixed application windows, the British Council Travel Grant Fund operates as a rolling fund, allowing eligible applicants to submit applications throughout the year before their intended travel.

About the British Council Travel Grant Fund

International festivals, film markets, labs, and co-production forums provide filmmakers with opportunities to:

  • Showcase their creative work to international audiences.
  • Meet producers, distributors, financiers, and collaborators.
  • Build long-term professional networks.
  • Discover international market opportunities.
  • Develop future film projects.
  • Increase the visibility of UK film and XR talent worldwide.
  • Learn from global industry experts.
  • Secure partnerships and funding for future productions.

The Travel Grant Fund was established to remove some of the financial barriers that prevent filmmakers and XR creatives from participating in these important international events.

The programme particularly focuses on increasing the international exposure of new and emerging UK filmmakers and XR creators, helping them build sustainable international careers.

What Does the Travel Grant Fund Support?

The fund supports travel to selected international events through five funding strands, grouped under two major application categories.

1. Festivals and Showcases

Applicants wishing to attend international festivals or showcase events can apply through one of the following grants.

Short Film Festival Grants

These grants are available for:

  • New and emerging writers
  • New and emerging directors
  • Producers of any experience level

The funding supports attendance at eligible international festivals where a short film is being screened.

This grant enables filmmakers to:

  • Attend film screenings.
  • Meet festival programmers.
  • Connect with distributors.
  • Build international industry relationships.
  • Promote their short films to wider audiences.

Feature Film Festival Grants

This funding is specifically designed for producers attending eligible international festivals where a feature film is being screened.

The grant helps producers:

  • Represent their feature film internationally.
  • Meet sales agents.
  • Connect with distributors.
  • Build financing opportunities.
  • Expand international partnerships.

Interactive, Immersive and XR Grants

This funding supports XR creatives attending eligible international festivals and showcase events with interactive, immersive, or XR projects.

Eligible projects may include:

  • Virtual Reality (VR)
  • Augmented Reality (AR)
  • Mixed Reality (MR)
  • Interactive storytelling
  • Immersive digital experiences

Recipients can showcase innovative work while building valuable international collaborations.

2. Labs and Co-Production Forums

The second application route focuses on supporting feature film development through international labs and co-production forums.

Lab Grants

Lab Grants are intended for:

  • Producers
  • Writers
  • Directors

Applicants must be working on new feature film projects and attending eligible international creative development labs.

These labs provide opportunities to:

  • Develop projects with experienced mentors.
  • Refine scripts.
  • Improve production strategies.
  • Receive professional feedback.
  • Build relationships with international industry professionals.

Co-Production Forum Grants

These grants are available for producers seeking international finance and production partners for feature film projects.

The funding supports attendance at eligible international markets and co-production forums where producers can:

  • Present projects to investors.
  • Meet international production partners.
  • Secure financing opportunities.
  • Expand international co-production networks.

Eligible International Events

Applicants can only receive funding for approved international events.

The British Council maintains Key Lists of eligible:

  • Short film festivals
  • Feature film festivals
  • XR festivals and showcase events
  • Creative development labs
  • Co-production forums

These lists are carefully curated to include:

  • Major international film events
  • Leading XR festivals
  • Industry-recognized development programmes
  • Specialist events supporting diverse creative voices

The Key Lists are reviewed every six months, and all travel awards remain subject to available funding.

Benefits of the British Council Travel Grant Fund

Successful applicants benefit in many ways beyond financial assistance.

Key benefits include:

  • Financial support for international travel.
  • Increased international exposure.
  • Opportunities to showcase films and XR projects.
  • Access to leading international festivals.
  • Professional networking opportunities.
  • Connections with producers, distributors, financiers, and collaborators.
  • Career development opportunities.
  • Participation in internationally recognized creative labs.
  • Access to co-production markets.
  • Increased visibility for UK creative talent.
  • Support for emerging filmmakers entering international markets.

Accessibility and Inclusive Support

The British Council is committed to making its funding programmes accessible to everyone.

Applicants requiring additional assistance can request support throughout the application process.

Available support includes:

  • Funding guidelines in alternative formats such as large print or audio.
  • Assistance understanding application guidelines.
  • Help completing application forms.
  • Additional travel funding for:
    • British Sign Language (BSL) interpreters.
    • Access workers.

Applicants with accessibility needs are encouraged to contact the British Council before submitting their application.

Support requests can be made by emailing:

Films@britishcouncil.org

Sustainability Commitment

The British Council recognizes that international travel contributes to environmental challenges. However, it also acknowledges that face-to-face participation remains invaluable for filmmakers and XR creatives, particularly those at the beginning of their careers.

To encourage more sustainable travel practices, the programme has partnered with Julie’s Bicycle to produce a Greener Travel Grants Guide.

Recipients travelling to European events are encouraged to use rail travel wherever possible.

Additional funding is also available to support train travel to eligible European events.

Funding Partners

The Travel Grant Fund is made possible through collaboration between:

  • BFI National Lottery
  • BFI NETWORK
  • BFI International Fund
  • British Council

Together, these organizations help strengthen the international reach of UK film and immersive creative industries.

Application Process

The Travel Grant Fund has been designed with a straightforward application process.

Applicants simply choose the funding strand that matches their project and event before submitting their application ahead of travel.

The two main application routes are:

Festivals and Showcases

  • Short Film Festival Grants
  • Feature Film Festival Grants
  • Interactive/Immersive/XR Grants

Labs and Co-Production Forums

  • Lab Grants
  • Co-Production Forum Grants

Applications are assessed according to eligibility requirements and available funding.

Important Deadline

There is no fixed application deadline.

The British Council Travel Grant Fund operates as a rolling funding programme, meaning applications are accepted throughout the year.

However, applicants must submit their application before travelling. Funding cannot be awarded for festivals, labs, showcases, or events that have already taken place.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in internationally recognized festivals, development labs, and co-production forums can transform a filmmaker’s career. Beyond financial assistance, the British Council Travel Grant Fund provides access to opportunities that foster international recognition, industry partnerships, creative growth, and future funding prospects.

Whether you are screening a short film, launching a feature, showcasing an immersive XR project, or developing your next production with global collaborators, this programme offers valuable support to help UK creative professionals reach wider audiences and build lasting international careers.

Interested applicants should review the eligible event lists, select the appropriate funding strand, and submit their application before making travel arrangements.

Click here for more and to apply

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post British Council Travel Grant Fund 2026: Funding for UK Filmmakers and XR Creatives to Attend International Festivals, Labs and Co-Production Forums appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Palladium Junior Professional Opportunity in Ghana 2026: Build Your Career in Sustainable Land Use, Climate, and International Development
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Palladium is recruiting a Junior Professional to support the West Africa team working on the Investments in Forests and Sustainable Land Use Programme – Phase 2 (IFSLU2). The successful candidate will be based primarily in: Location: Accra, GhanaWork arrangement: Full-time position (40 hours per week)Expected start date: October 1, 2026Programme duration: One-year professional development opportunity […]

The post Palladium Junior Professional Opportunity in Ghana 2026: Build Your Career in Sustainable Land Use, Climate, and International Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Palladium is recruiting a Junior Professional to support the West Africa team working on the Investments in Forests and Sustainable Land Use Programme – Phase 2 (IFSLU2).

The successful candidate will be based primarily in:

Location: Accra, Ghana
Work arrangement: Full-time position (40 hours per week)
Expected start date: October 1, 2026
Programme duration: One-year professional development opportunity

The role provides hands-on experience in:

  • Sustainable land-use projects
  • Climate and environmental programmes
  • Investment development
  • Business incubation
  • Grant management
  • Research and analysis
  • Project coordination
  • Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL)

The Junior Professional will work closely with teams responsible for grants, communications, monitoring, evaluation, learning, and external relations.

About the Investments in Forests and Sustainable Land Use Programme – Phase 2 (IFSLU2)

The IFSLU2 programme is one of Palladium’s flagship initiatives under its Natural Capital and Climate, Environment and Natural Resources practice areas.

The programme aims to support businesses, investors, governments, and communities in developing sustainable economic opportunities connected to forests and land use.

The initiative focuses on creating investment models where economic growth can happen while also:

  • Protecting existing forests
  • Restoring degraded land
  • Improving agricultural production practices
  • Supporting sustainable businesses
  • Increasing private-sector investment in environmentally responsible activities

The programme operates across tropical forest regions and is funded by the United Kingdom government through the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO).

IFSLU2 began in February 2025 and is planned to operate for five years, with possible extension depending on performance and funding availability.

Key Responsibilities of the Junior Professional

The selected candidate will support the West Africa team in several important areas.

1. Project Development Support

Responsibilities include:

  • Conducting market research and value-chain analysis to support sustainable business projects.
  • Supporting business diagnostics and feasibility assessments.
  • Preparing business plans and economic analyses.
  • Evaluating investment opportunities and project sustainability.
  • Supporting innovative solutions that encourage private-sector investment in forestry and sustainable land use.
  • Assisting with the preparation of project concept notes and idea documents.
  • Reviewing project proposals submitted by partners and improving them according to programme requirements.
  • Supporting the inclusion of gender considerations and safeguarding requirements in project planning.
  • Working with monitoring and evaluation teams to identify expected project outcomes.
  • Participating in stakeholder meetings and documenting important discussions.

2. Project Management Support

The Junior Professional will assist with effective project implementation through activities such as:

  • Supporting project monitoring and reporting.
  • Reviewing technical and financial reports.
  • Working with grant recipients to resolve reporting challenges.
  • Supporting payment processes, including verifying payment information and preparing documentation.
  • Helping identify lessons learned and impact stories from projects.
  • Maintaining communication with selected project partners.
  • Supporting regular engagement and problem-solving activities.

3. Reporting and Programme Coordination

The role will also involve:

  • Supporting the preparation of client reports and internal programme updates.
  • Organising meetings, events, and coordination activities.
  • Preparing documentation for project approval committees.
  • Maintaining programme records and information systems.
  • Uploading research documents and reports into platforms such as SharePoint and Monday.com.
  • Supporting quarterly, annual, and special reporting requirements.
  • Contributing to technical reports and programme deliverables.

Required Qualifications and Skills

Applicants should demonstrate strong academic preparation and professional potential.

Educational Requirements

Candidates should have a:

  • Bachelor’s degree or Master’s degree in:
    • Economics
    • Finance
    • Agribusiness
    • Environmental Economics
    • International Development
    • Environmental Studies
    • Public Policy
    • International Relations
    • Related fields

Technical Skills Required

Successful applicants should have:

  • Strong analytical and research abilities.
  • Experience conducting financial analysis or market assessments.
  • Ability to prepare business plans, feasibility studies, or investment cases.
  • Strong Microsoft Office skills, especially:
    • Microsoft Excel
    • Microsoft PowerPoint
  • Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.
  • Strong organisational skills and attention to detail.

Personal Qualities

Palladium is seeking candidates who are:

  • Adaptable and open to new approaches.
  • Innovative problem-solvers.
  • Strong communicators.
  • Able to build professional relationships.
  • Responsible and accountable.
  • Passionate about climate, sustainability, and international development.

Additional Preferred Qualifications

Although not mandatory, candidates with the following experience may have an advantage:

  • Experience working with international development organisations.
  • Experience supporting FCDO-funded programmes.
  • Background in environmental studies or sustainable agriculture.
  • Knowledge of forest-risk commodities such as:
    • Rubber
    • Cashew
    • Oil palm
  • French language skills.

Benefits of This Opportunity

This Junior Professional position provides several career-development benefits, including:

1. Professional Growth

Participants gain practical experience working on an international development programme while developing skills needed for careers in sustainability and global development.

2. Exposure to Global Projects

The role provides experience working with:

  • International development specialists
  • Environmental professionals
  • Investment teams
  • Grant management experts
  • Monitoring and evaluation specialists

3. Career Development Skills

Participants can strengthen abilities in:

  • Research
  • Business analysis
  • Project management
  • Reporting
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Sustainable investment planning

4. Contribution to Environmental Sustainability

The opportunity allows professionals to contribute to projects focused on:

  • Forest protection
  • Climate resilience
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Responsible investment

Diversity, Inclusion, and Safeguarding Commitment

Palladium promotes equity, diversity, and inclusion throughout its recruitment process.

The organisation welcomes applications from people regardless of:

  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • Gender identity
  • Religion
  • Disability
  • Nationality
  • Socioeconomic background
  • Age
  • Other personal characteristics

Palladium also provides reasonable adjustments during recruitment for candidates requiring accommodations.

All successful candidates will participate in safeguarding-focused recruitment procedures and due diligence checks.

Deadline and Application Information

Application deadline: Not specified in the opportunity announcement. Applicants are encouraged to apply early.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more Global Internship Opportunities here.

The post Palladium Junior Professional Opportunity in Ghana 2026: Build Your Career in Sustainable Land Use, Climate, and International Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Supercell Developer Grants Program 2026: Equity-Free Grants of USD 20,000–200,000 for African Game Development Studios
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Applications are now open for the inaugural Supercell Developer Grants Program 2026, an exciting funding initiative designed to support exceptional game development studios across Africa. Through this new program, Supercell will provide equity-free (non-dilutive) grants ranging from USD 20,000 to USD 200,000 to help promising studios accelerate their growth, develop outstanding games, and strengthen Africa’s […]

The post Supercell Developer Grants Program 2026: Equity-Free Grants of USD 20,000–200,000 for African Game Development Studios appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the inaugural Supercell Developer Grants Program 2026, an exciting funding initiative designed to support exceptional game development studios across Africa. Through this new program, Supercell will provide equity-free (non-dilutive) grants ranging from USD 20,000 to USD 200,000 to help promising studios accelerate their growth, develop outstanding games, and strengthen Africa’s rapidly expanding gaming ecosystem.

Known globally for creating blockbuster mobile games such as Clash of Clans, Clash Royale, Brawl Stars, and Hay Day, Supercell is making a significant investment in Africa’s creative technology sector. The company believes that some of the world’s most innovative storytelling, artistic creativity, and game development talent are emerging from the African continent and hopes to support studios that will shape the future of the global gaming industry.

Unlike investment funding, these grants are non-dilutive, meaning successful applicants retain 100% ownership of their studios, games, and intellectual property (IP). The program is focused on supporting studios rather than individual projects, with funding intended to help businesses reach their next stage of sustainable growth.

About the Supercell Developer Grants Program

The Supercell Developer Grants Program was created to strengthen Africa’s emerging games ecosystem by supporting outstanding game development studios with flexible, equity-free funding.

Rather than investing in a single game, Supercell is investing in talented teams capable of building sustainable studios and producing high-quality games over the long term.

The program welcomes applications from studios developing games across all genres, platforms, and business models. Whether your studio develops mobile, PC, console, or cross-platform games, you are encouraged to apply if you have a compelling vision and a clear plan for growth.

For this first cohort, Supercell is primarily looking for:

  • Exceptional development teams
  • Innovative and engaging games
  • Original creative vision
  • Sustainable business potential
  • Clear plans for utilizing grant funding effectively

Why Supercell Created This Program

Supercell believes that great teams create great games.

As the gaming industry continues to evolve globally, Africa has become one of the fastest-growing regions for game development, with increasing numbers of talented studios creating original content inspired by African cultures, stories, and creativity.

Through this grants program, Supercell aims to:

  • Support Africa’s most promising game development studios.
  • Help studios scale their operations.
  • Encourage innovation in game development.
  • Foster long-term growth within Africa’s gaming ecosystem.
  • Build lasting relationships with talented development teams.
  • Learn alongside African creators while contributing to the industry’s future.

Grant Funding

Selected studios may receive equity-free grants ranging between USD 20,000 and USD 200,000, depending on their stage of development, funding needs, and growth plans.

The grants are designed to help studios reach meaningful development milestones while maintaining complete independence.

Most importantly:

  • Supercell does not take equity.
  • Supercell does not take ownership of your studio.
  • Supercell does not take ownership of your games.
  • Your studio retains full ownership of all intellectual property.

What Can the Grant Be Used For?

Successful studios may use grant funding for a wide range of game development and business activities that directly support their growth.

Eligible expenses include:

  • Salaries
  • Contractor payments
  • Art and animation
  • Game design
  • Software engineering
  • Audio production
  • Quality assurance (QA)
  • Software licenses and development tools
  • Marketing
  • Live game operations
  • Other game development-related expenses

The funding should be used to advance the studio and the game or development milestone identified in the application.

What Cannot Be Funded?

Grant funding should not be used for:

  • Personal expenses
  • Activities unrelated to the studio
  • Non-development business expenses

Further funding conditions will be discussed with successful applicants during the grant agreement process.

Who Can Apply?

The Supercell Developer Grants Program is specifically designed for legally registered game development studios operating in Africa.

To be eligible, applicants must:

  • Apply on behalf of a legally registered game development studio.
  • Have the studio’s primary operations based in Africa.
  • Have the majority of the studio team located in Africa.
  • Be willing to complete any required due diligence or compliance procedures if shortlisted.
  • Confirm that all information submitted is accurate.
  • Have the legal rights and permissions to submit all application materials.

Who Is Not Eligible?

The following are not eligible:

  • Individual developers
  • Hobby projects
  • Unregistered teams
  • Informal game development groups

Eligible Regions

The inaugural cohort is focused exclusively on Africa.

Studios from eligible African countries are encouraged to apply.

Studios with holding companies located outside Africa may still apply, provided their primary operations and majority of their teams are based within Africa.

Types of Games Accepted

Supercell welcomes a broad range of game development projects.

Applications are accepted from studios developing:

  • Mobile games
  • PC games
  • Console games
  • Cross-platform games
  • Indie games
  • Multiplayer games
  • Premium games
  • Free-to-play games
  • Hybrid business models
  • Other innovative gaming experiences

Applicants may include multiple games in their portfolio but should identify one primary game that the funding will support.

Application Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be reviewed by a cross-functional Supercell team.

Studios will be evaluated across five key areas:

1. Team

Supercell places strong emphasis on the quality, experience, and capability of the development team.

2. Game & Creative Vision

Reviewers will assess:

  • Originality
  • Creativity
  • Quality
  • Innovation
  • Long-term vision

3. Community & Audience

Applications should demonstrate evidence that players are already engaging with the studio’s games.

This may include:

  • Active communities
  • User feedback
  • Downloads
  • Player engagement
  • Early traction

4. Potential for Impact

Applications should explain how funding will help:

  • Grow the studio
  • Build sustainable operations
  • Strengthen Africa’s games ecosystem

5. Viability & Use of Funds

Applicants should clearly explain:

  • Funding requirements
  • Budget allocation
  • Development roadmap
  • Expected milestones

Application Requirements

Applicants should prepare the following before beginning the application:

  • Pitch deck
  • Gameplay trailer or gameplay video
  • Playable game build (optional)
  • Links to previous games
  • High-level funding plan
  • Budget

The application takes approximately one hour to complete.

Application Timeline

The first cohort follows the timeline below:

  • Applications Open: Monday, 6 July 2026
  • Applications Close: Sunday, 9 August 2026 (23:59 UTC)
  • Review Period: August–October 2026
  • Successful Applicants Notified: October 2026
  • Grant Funding Begins: December 2026

Supercell notes that these dates may change depending on the volume of applications and the review process.

Selection Process

The review process may include:

  1. Initial application review.
  2. Cross-functional evaluation.
  3. Shortlisting.
  4. Additional documentation.
  5. Due diligence.
  6. Interviews (for selected applicants).
  7. Final selection.

Approximately 3–5 studios are expected to receive grants during the first cohort.

Successful Applicants

Selected studios will:

  • Enter into a formal grant agreement.
  • Receive funding according to agreed milestones.
  • Submit periodic progress updates.
  • Potentially be publicly announced as grant recipients.

Future Funding Rounds

Supercell is currently focused on launching the inaugural cohort.

Future funding rounds may be announced through the company’s official website and communication channels.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Africa’s gaming industry is experiencing rapid growth, with increasing investment, expanding talent, and a rising global audience.

The Supercell Developer Grants Program represents one of the most significant equity-free funding opportunities currently available to African game development studios.

Beyond financial support, the initiative demonstrates international confidence in Africa’s creative technology sector and provides studios with an opportunity to accelerate development while maintaining complete ownership of their businesses and intellectual property.

How to Apply

Interested studios should complete the online application before the deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the eligibility requirements, prepare all required documents, and present a clear vision for how the grant will help their studio reach its next stage of growth.

Apply Here

Deadline

Application Deadline: 9 August 2026 (23:59 UTC)

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Supercell Developer Grants Program 2026: Equity-Free Grants of USD 20,000–200,000 for African Game Development Studios appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Applications: UNICEF Pre-Conference of the Parties 31 Australia and Pacific Youth Climate Dialogue 2026 in Fiji (Fully Funded)
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Climate change continues to be one of the world’s most pressing challenges, and young people are increasingly taking center stage in shaping climate policies and solutions. To amplify youth voices ahead of the 31st Conference of the Parties (COP31), UNICEF, in collaboration with the Governments of Fiji and Australia and several regional partners, has opened […]

The post Call for Applications: UNICEF Pre-Conference of the Parties 31 Australia and Pacific Youth Climate Dialogue 2026 in Fiji (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Climate change continues to be one of the world’s most pressing challenges, and young people are increasingly taking center stage in shaping climate policies and solutions. To amplify youth voices ahead of the 31st Conference of the Parties (COP31), UNICEF, in collaboration with the Governments of Fiji and Australia and several regional partners, has opened applications for the Pre-Conference of the Parties 31 Australia and Pacific Youth Climate Dialogue (Pre-COP31 APYCD) 2026.

The fully funded regional dialogue will bring together more than 60 young climate leaders aged 14 to 29 years from Australia, New Zealand, Pacific Island nations, and surrounding regions. Scheduled to take place in Nadi, Fiji, from 1–3 October 2026, the event will provide participants with a unique opportunity to influence regional and international climate discussions while strengthening their leadership, advocacy, and networking skills.

If you are passionate about climate action and want your voice to contribute to decisions that shape the future of the planet, this is an opportunity you should not miss.

About the UNICEF Pre-Conference of the Parties 31 Australia and Pacific Youth Climate Dialogue

The Pre-Conference of the Parties 31 Australia and Pacific Youth Climate Dialogue (Pre-COP31 APYCD) is a regional platform created to ensure that children and young people have a meaningful role in climate policymaking before the official COP31 negotiations.

The dialogue serves as a bridge between youth perspectives and high-level climate negotiations. Participants will discuss climate challenges affecting their communities, develop practical recommendations, and collaborate with fellow youth leaders to shape regional priorities before they are presented during Pre-COP31 and ultimately at COP31.

Unlike many conferences where young people simply attend as observers, this dialogue is intentionally designed by young people and for young people, ensuring that their experiences, ideas, and recommendations directly influence climate discussions among governments and negotiators.

The 2026 dialogue will be held immediately before the Pacific Pre-COP31 meeting, allowing youth recommendations to feed directly into official regional and global climate negotiations.

Organizations Behind the Dialogue

The dialogue is organized through a strong regional partnership involving:

  • UNICEF
  • Government of Fiji
  • Government of Australia
  • Pacific Island Students Fighting Climate Change
  • Pacific Islands Climate Action Network
  • Pacific Youth Council

Additional supporting partners include:

  • Secretariat of the Pacific Regional Environment Programme
  • Pacific Islands Forum Secretariat
  • Pacific Community
  • Pacific Disability Forum

This collaboration demonstrates a shared commitment to ensuring that youth voices remain central to climate governance across the Pacific region.

What Participants Will Do

Successful applicants will participate in a three-day intensive regional dialogue in Fiji where they will engage in various learning, collaboration, and policy development activities.

Participants will:

  • Share their experiences and perspectives on climate change affecting their communities.
  • Develop youth-led recommendations for Pre-COP31 and COP31.
  • Collaborate with young climate leaders from across Australia, New Zealand, Pacific Island nations, and other participating regions.
  • Build regional and international professional networks.
  • Participate in interactive sessions on:
    • Climate policy
    • Climate advocacy
    • Leadership
    • Climate negotiations
  • Learn directly from climate experts and regional stakeholders.
  • Engage with policymakers and negotiators responsible for climate decision-making.
  • Strengthen long-term collaboration among youth organizations throughout the Pacific region.
  • Gain practical experience that supports continued participation in regional and international climate action initiatives.

Benefits of Participating

Being selected for the dialogue offers much more than attending a conference. Participants will receive valuable leadership development while contributing to real climate policy discussions.

Some of the major benefits include:

Fully Funded Participation

UNICEF will directly cover:

  • International travel
  • Domestic travel where applicable
  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Event participation costs
  • Chaperone costs for participants under 18 years of age

Importantly, participants will not be required to pay these expenses upfront or seek reimbursement.

Leadership Development

Participants will strengthen their skills in:

  • Climate leadership
  • Policy advocacy
  • Public speaking
  • Negotiation
  • Collaborative decision-making

International Networking

Participants will build relationships with:

  • Youth leaders
  • Climate activists
  • Government representatives
  • United Nations agencies
  • Civil society organizations
  • Regional organizations
  • Development partners

These connections can lead to future collaborations, fellowships, conferences, and leadership opportunities.

Opportunity to Influence Climate Policy

One of the most significant benefits is the opportunity to ensure youth priorities are reflected in discussions leading to Pre-COP31 and COP31.

Participants will help shape recommendations that may influence future climate policies affecting the Pacific region and beyond.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The organizers are committed to ensuring broad representation from across the region.

Applications are especially encouraged from:

  • Young women
  • Adolescents
  • Indigenous youth
  • First Nations youth
  • Persons with disabilities
  • LGBTQI+ youth
  • Young people from remote communities
  • Young people from underserved communities

The organizers aim to create a dialogue that reflects the diverse cultures, backgrounds, and lived experiences of young people across Australia and the Pacific.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements:

  • Be between 14 and 29 years old during the event (1–3 October 2026).
  • Be a resident of one of the eligible participating countries.
  • Be available to travel and participate in person in Nadi, Fiji.
  • Be able to communicate effectively in English.

The organizers will also provide sign language interpretation where required to ensure accessibility.

Participants should note that travel schedules may require arrival before the event or departure after the event depending on available flights.

Selection Criteria

Applicants will be assessed based on several factors, including:

Commitment to Climate Action

Applicants should demonstrate genuine interest in:

  • Climate change
  • Environmental protection
  • Sustainability
  • Climate resilience

This commitment may be demonstrated through personal experiences, learning, volunteer work, or advocacy.

Active Participation

Preference will be given to applicants who have participated in climate-related activities such as:

  • School initiatives
  • Community projects
  • National campaigns
  • Regional programs
  • International climate activities

Youth Representation

Applicants should be connected to youth groups, organizations, schools, or community networks and demonstrate the ability to represent broader youth perspectives rather than only personal opinions.

Leadership

The organizers are looking for young people who:

  • Take initiative
  • Inspire others
  • Lead community projects
  • Demonstrate problem-solving abilities

Leadership experience does not have to be formal.

Collaboration Skills

Successful applicants should be able to:

  • Communicate effectively
  • Listen respectfully
  • Work with diverse groups
  • Participate constructively in discussions

Travel Insurance

While UNICEF covers the major participation expenses, participants are responsible for obtaining their own travel insurance.

Travel insurance should ideally include coverage for:

  • Medical emergencies
  • Travel disruptions
  • Accidents
  • Lost belongings

Applicants are strongly encouraged to purchase suitable insurance before travelling.

Application Process

Applicants are required to complete an online application form before the applicable deadline.

The application requires:

  • Personal information
  • Background details
  • Relevant experience
  • Motivation for participating
  • Responses to two video questions

Applicants may record their video responses in advance and upload them during the application process.

Reasonable adjustments are available for persons with disabilities.

Application Deadline

Applicants must submit their applications before:

  • 26 July 2026 – Pacific Island nation applicants
  • 30 July 2026 – Australia and New Zealand applicants

Late applications are unlikely to be considered.

Conclusion

The UNICEF Pre-Conference of the Parties 31 Australia and Pacific Youth Climate Dialogue 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for young climate leaders to contribute directly to regional and global climate policymaking.

Beyond the fully funded travel to Fiji, participants will gain valuable leadership skills, expand their professional networks, collaborate with fellow youth advocates, and ensure that the voices of young people are reflected in discussions leading to COP31.

If you are passionate about climate action and meet the eligibility requirements, this dialogue offers an excellent platform to make your voice heard while developing the skills and connections needed to become a future climate leader.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more Global Conference Opportunities here.

The post Call for Applications: UNICEF Pre-Conference of the Parties 31 Australia and Pacific Youth Climate Dialogue 2026 in Fiji (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Gadfly Project Custom Web Application Grant 2026: Apply for In-Kind Software Development Support Worth Up to US$100,000
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Organizations around the world looking to build innovative digital solutions can now apply for The Gadfly Project Custom Web Application Grant 2026. This unique opportunity provides in-kind software development support instead of direct financial funding, enabling eligible organizations to transform technology ideas into fully functional software applications. Unlike traditional grants that provide cash awards, The […]

The post The Gadfly Project Custom Web Application Grant 2026: Apply for In-Kind Software Development Support Worth Up to US$100,000 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations around the world looking to build innovative digital solutions can now apply for The Gadfly Project Custom Web Application Grant 2026. This unique opportunity provides in-kind software development support instead of direct financial funding, enabling eligible organizations to transform technology ideas into fully functional software applications.

Unlike traditional grants that provide cash awards, The Gadfly Project contributes its expertise by assigning its experienced in-house software development team to work directly with selected organizations. Successful applicants receive professional technical support to design, build, or improve digital tools that strengthen organizational operations, expand community engagement, and increase social impact.

The estimated value of this software development support ranges from US$5,000 to US$100,000, making it an excellent opportunity for nonprofits, charities, social enterprises, community organizations, and mission-driven institutions that need technology solutions but may lack the financial resources to hire professional developers.

Whether your organization wants to create a custom web application, improve internal systems, build an outreach platform, or develop a database that enhances efficiency, this grant could provide the technical expertise needed to bring your vision to life.

About The Gadfly Project

The Gadfly Project is committed to supporting organizations that create positive change through technology. Rather than offering cash grants, the organization partners with mission-driven organizations by providing professional software development services that help solve operational challenges and expand community impact.

The project’s philosophy is simple: technology should empower organizations to work more efficiently, serve more people, and create lasting positive outcomes.

Through its Custom Web Application Grant, The Gadfly Project helps organizations leverage technology as a strategic tool for growth, innovation, and sustainable impact.

What is the Custom Web Application Grant?

The Custom Web Application Grant is an in-kind technology grant that provides organizations with software development expertise from The Gadfly Project’s internal development team.

Instead of receiving money, selected applicants receive professional assistance to build custom software solutions tailored to their organizational needs.

This means organizations can access high-quality software development services without paying commercial development costs.

What Types of Projects Are Supported?

The grant supports a wide variety of technology projects designed to improve organizational performance and community impact.

Eligible project ideas include:

1. Custom Web Applications

Organizations can build web-based platforms designed specifically for their programs, beneficiaries, staff, or stakeholders.

2. Databases

Applicants can develop custom databases that improve data management, reporting, and organizational decision-making.

3. Internal Operations Platforms

The grant can support software that streamlines internal workflows, staff collaboration, project management, or administrative operations.

4. Outreach and Supporter Engagement Tools

Organizations may build digital platforms that strengthen communication with donors, volunteers, members, supporters, or beneficiaries.

5. Organizational Efficiency Software

Custom digital systems that automate repetitive processes, improve productivity, or reduce operational costs are encouraged.

6. Technology That Scales Impact

Organizations with innovative ideas capable of serving larger audiences or increasing program effectiveness are strongly encouraged to apply.

Who Should Apply?

The Gadfly Project welcomes applications from organizations committed to creating positive social or environmental impact.

Ideal applicants include organizations working to improve:

  • Human well-being
  • Environmental conservation
  • Community development
  • Social innovation
  • Organizational effectiveness
  • Public engagement
  • Outreach and communication

Applicants should clearly demonstrate how the proposed software solution will benefit users, strengthen operations, or improve service delivery.

The organization places significant emphasis on understanding the potential reach of the technology rather than simply the project’s technical complexity.

Grant Value

Selected organizations receive software development services with an estimated value ranging between:

  • US$5,000
  • US$100,000

The final value depends on the complexity and scope of the project.

Importantly, The Gadfly Project emphasizes that project size alone does not determine selection. Instead, reviewers consider factors such as:

  • Number of users served
  • Community impact
  • Potential for long-term sustainability
  • Ability to improve organizational effectiveness
  • Expected benefits for beneficiaries

This means that even relatively small software projects with significant community impact have strong potential for selection.

Benefits of the Grant

Receiving this grant offers numerous advantages for organizations seeking digital transformation.

Key benefits include:

  • Professional software development support
  • Access to an experienced in-house development team
  • No software development costs for selected projects
  • Opportunity to transform ideas into working technology
  • Improved organizational efficiency
  • Better stakeholder engagement
  • Enhanced service delivery
  • Greater operational scalability
  • Stronger digital infrastructure
  • Increased long-term organizational impact
