Organizations that lack internal technical expertise can particularly benefit from working directly with experienced developers throughout the project lifecycle.

How Applications Are Reviewed

Applications are reviewed on a quarterly basis, providing multiple opportunities throughout the year for organizations to apply.

Following submission, applicants can generally expect to receive a response within approximately two weeks after the application deadline.

Organizations are encouraged to prepare thoughtful applications that clearly explain:

  • The problem they want to solve
  • The proposed software solution
  • Expected users
  • Community impact
  • Long-term sustainability
  • Organizational goals

Well-defined project concepts are likely to strengthen an application’s competitiveness.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Technology has become an essential driver of organizational effectiveness. Unfortunately, many nonprofit and mission-driven organizations lack the financial resources needed to build custom digital solutions.

The Gadfly Project addresses this challenge by providing technical expertise instead of financial assistance, allowing organizations to access professional software development that might otherwise be beyond their budget.

Whether your organization serves communities, protects the environment, improves healthcare, supports education, or promotes social justice, investing in digital infrastructure can significantly increase your reach and effectiveness.

This grant represents an excellent opportunity for organizations ready to embrace technology as a catalyst for lasting social impact.

Application Deadlines (2026):

  • 24 July 2026
  • 23 October 2026

Applications should be submitted by midnight on the relevant quarterly deadline. Successful applicants can expect a response within approximately two weeks.

Apply Here

Learn more about this opportunity on this link

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY here

The post The Gadfly Project Custom Web Application Grant 2026: Apply for In-Kind Software Development Support Worth Up to US$100,000 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GrowthAfrica Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate Job 2026: Join a Leading Pan-African Organisation Advancing Inclusive Entrepreneurship
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
GrowthAfrica is inviting qualified and passionate professionals to apply for the position of Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate based in Nairobi, Kenya. This exciting onsite opportunity offers experienced professionals the chance to contribute to one of Africa’s leading entrepreneurship support organizations by integrating gender equality, inclusion, and safeguarding into a flagship entrepreneurship programme operating across […]

The post GrowthAfrica Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate Job 2026: Join a Leading Pan-African Organisation Advancing Inclusive Entrepreneurship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
GrowthAfrica is inviting qualified and passionate professionals to apply for the position of Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate based in Nairobi, Kenya. This exciting onsite opportunity offers experienced professionals the chance to contribute to one of Africa’s leading entrepreneurship support organizations by integrating gender equality, inclusion, and safeguarding into a flagship entrepreneurship programme operating across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana.

If you are passionate about creating equitable opportunities, strengthening safeguarding systems, and promoting inclusive entrepreneurship, this role provides a unique opportunity to influence programmes that empower entrepreneurs across multiple African countries.

Working with GrowthAfrica means becoming part of a purpose-driven international team committed to supporting entrepreneurs, fostering innovation, and building stronger entrepreneurship ecosystems where everyone has an equal opportunity to succeed.

About GrowthAfrica

GrowthAfrica is a leading African organization dedicated to supporting entrepreneurs, startups, and small businesses through business acceleration, ecosystem development, investment readiness, and innovation programmes.

The organization collaborates with entrepreneurs, development partners, governments, investors, and private sector stakeholders to strengthen entrepreneurial ecosystems across Africa. Through its programmes, GrowthAfrica promotes inclusive economic development by supporting businesses that create jobs, solve local challenges, and contribute to sustainable growth.

A core part of GrowthAfrica’s mission is ensuring that entrepreneurship programmes are accessible, equitable, and safe for all participants regardless of gender or background.

About the Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate Role

The Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate will play a central role in embedding gender equality, diversity, inclusion, and safeguarding principles throughout a flagship entrepreneurship programme operating across four African countries.

The successful candidate will work closely with programme teams, implementing partners, and stakeholders to ensure that entrepreneurship initiatives create respectful, safe, and inclusive environments for participants.

This is an excellent opportunity for professionals interested in gender equality, safeguarding, international development, programme management, and entrepreneurship ecosystem development.

Job Details

  • Position: Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate
  • Organization: GrowthAfrica
  • Location: Nairobi, Kenya
  • Work Arrangement: Onsite
  • Application Deadline: 19 July 2026

Why This Role Matters

Entrepreneurship has the potential to transform lives and economies, but many entrepreneurs continue to face barriers related to gender inequality, discrimination, exclusion, and unsafe learning environments.

GrowthAfrica recognizes that entrepreneurship ecosystems become stronger when all individuals can participate equally and safely.

The Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate will help ensure that entrepreneurship programmes:

  • Promote equal participation.
  • Reduce barriers to inclusion.
  • Strengthen safeguarding mechanisms.
  • Foster respectful learning environments.
  • Improve access to opportunities for underrepresented groups.
  • Enhance programme quality across multiple countries.

By strengthening these systems, the successful candidate will directly contribute to more inclusive entrepreneurship ecosystems throughout Africa.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will lead several important initiatives aimed at strengthening inclusion and safeguarding across GrowthAfrica’s programmes.

Responsibilities include:

1. Lead Gender Mainstreaming

Integrate gender equality principles throughout programme planning, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation.

2. Strengthen Safeguarding Systems

Develop and improve safeguarding policies, reporting procedures, and response mechanisms that protect programme participants.

3. Conduct Gender and Inclusion Analysis

Assess programme activities to identify barriers affecting participation and recommend strategies that improve inclusion.

4. Train Programme Teams and Partners

Design and deliver training sessions that build staff and partner capacity on gender equality, safeguarding, and inclusive programming.

5. Monitor Inclusion Outcomes

Track programme performance by measuring inclusion indicators, safeguarding compliance, and overall effectiveness.

6. Support Donor Reporting

Prepare reports demonstrating programme achievements, safeguarding performance, and gender mainstreaming outcomes for funding partners.

7. Contribute to Organisational Learning

Document lessons learned and recommend improvements that strengthen GrowthAfrica’s long-term organisational practices.

Benefits of Working with GrowthAfrica

GrowthAfrica offers more than employment—it provides an opportunity to influence entrepreneurship development across Africa.

Benefits include:

  • Meaningful work that creates lasting social impact.
  • Opportunity to shape inclusive entrepreneurship programmes.
  • Exposure to entrepreneurship ecosystems across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ghana.
  • Work alongside an experienced international team.
  • Competitive salary and employee benefits.
  • Professional growth within a respected African development organisation.
  • Opportunity to influence organisational policy and practice.

Who Should Apply?

This role is suitable for professionals with experience or interest in:

  • Gender equality
  • Safeguarding
  • Social inclusion
  • International development
  • Entrepreneurship development
  • Programme management
  • Community development
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Capacity building
  • Donor-funded projects

Applicants should be passionate about ensuring that entrepreneurship programmes remain accessible, equitable, and safe for all participants.

Why You Should Apply

Inclusive entrepreneurship has become a major priority for governments, development organisations, investors, and international donors across Africa.

Professionals with expertise in gender mainstreaming and safeguarding are increasingly needed to design programmes that remove barriers and improve participation among women, youth, and other underserved populations.

Joining GrowthAfrica provides an opportunity to contribute to initiatives that strengthen entrepreneurship ecosystems while building valuable experience working across multiple African countries.

If you are motivated by social impact, inclusion, and sustainable economic development, this position offers a rewarding opportunity to help shape programmes that empower entrepreneurs and create lasting change.

Application Deadline: 19 July 2026

Click here for Full Job Description

Apply Here

Discover more global Job opportunities of OFY here

The post GrowthAfrica Gender Mainstreaming & Safeguarding Associate Job 2026: Join a Leading Pan-African Organisation Advancing Inclusive Entrepreneurship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
